statusLine: EU fuel security remains under strain with multiple countries critically below mandatory 90-day reserves despite rising oil prices hitting $109 per barrel.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security shows alarming weaknesses across multiple member states, with 10 countries holding critical petrol reserves and 12 countries below safe diesel levels. Romania faces the most severe crisis with only 20.2 days of diesel reserves—less than a quarter of the mandatory 90-day minimum. Poland, Spain, and Luxembourg also show critical shortages across all three fuel types, indicating systemic supply chain stress.

The situation is compounded by Brent crude oil surging to $109.03 per barrel, up 7.78% recently and approximately 27% since Middle East conflicts began disrupting global supplies. This price pressure, combined with strategic reserve releases by several EU countries, has created a perfect storm of reduced stockpiles and elevated costs. Average EU fuel prices reflect this strain, with diesel at €2.00 per liter and significant regional disparities—Netherlands petrol costs €2.334 versus Malta's €1.34.

While EU averages suggest marginal compliance (petrol 94.5 days, diesel 95.8 days), these figures mask dangerous fragmentation. Eastern European countries like Romania, Poland, and Lithuania show particularly acute vulnerabilities, while even major economies like Germany and France hold critical reserves in key fuel categories. The jet fuel situation is especially concerning, with the EU average at 89.9 days—just below the safety threshold—and 13 countries in critical status.
keyPoints: Romania holds only 20.2 days of diesel reserves, creating an immediate fuel security emergency well below the 90-day EU mandate
Ten EU countries maintain critical petrol reserves and 12 hold critical diesel stocks, indicating widespread supply vulnerabilities
Brent crude oil at $109.03 per barrel represents a 27% increase since Middle East conflicts began, driving fuel costs higher across Europe
Jet fuel reserves average 89.9 days EU-wide—below the 90-day safety threshold—with 13 countries in critical status threatening aviation sector stability
Eastern European nations show the most severe shortages, with Poland, Romania, and Lithuania facing critical reserves across multiple fuel types
